facts.text: The Godfather (1972) was initially disliked by Paramount executives, who considered firing Francis Ford Coppola multiple times during production.
Meryl Streep holds the record for the most Academy Award nominations of any actor, with 21 nominations over her career.
Breaking Bad's pilot episode was filmed in Albuquerque, New Mexico, which became a character in its own right throughout the series.
Roger Deakins, one of cinema's most celebrated cinematographers, won his first Oscar for Blade Runner 2049 after 14 nominations.
Citizen Kane (1941) was co-written and directed by Orson Welles when he was just 25 years old.
Aaron Sorkin is known for his rapid-fire dialogue style, often called 'walk and talk' scenes, first popularized in The West Wing.
Daniel Day-Lewis is the only actor to have won three Best Actor Oscars: for My Left Foot, There Will Be Blood, and Lincoln.
The Sopranos is widely credited with launching the golden age of television drama when it premiered on HBO in 1999.
Parasite (2019) became the first non-English language film to win the Academy Award for Best Picture.
Martin Scorsese and Robert De Niro have collaborated on nine films together, starting with Mean Streets in 1973.
The Shawshank Redemption (1994) was a box office disappointment but became one of the most beloved films ever after its home video release.
Hans Zimmer's score for Inception used an extremely slowed-down version of Édith Piaf's 'Non, je ne regrette rien' as its basis.
Casablanca (1942) was based on an unproduced stage play called 'Everybody Comes to Rick's.'
The Wire creator David Simon was a former Baltimore Sun reporter who drew heavily on his journalism experience for the show.
Denzel Washington has won two Academy Awards: Best Supporting Actor for Glory and Best Actor for Training Day.
Schindler's List (1993) was filmed almost entirely in black and white, with Steven Spielberg choosing not to take a salary for the film.
Better Call Saul ran for six seasons and is considered one of the rare prequels that rivals or surpasses the original series.
Francis Ford Coppola mortgaged his own home to help finance Apocalypse Now, which went massively over budget.
Moonlight (2016) was made on a budget of just $1.5 million and went on to win Best Picture at the Academy Awards.
12 Angry Men (1957) takes place almost entirely in a single jury deliberation room, yet remains one of the most gripping films ever made.
The Crown has recast its entire principal cast twice to reflect the aging of the British royal family over decades.
Ennio Morricone composed over 400 film scores during his career, including The Good, the Bad and the Ugly and Cinema Paradiso.
David Fincher is known for his perfectionism, sometimes requiring 50 or more takes of a single scene during production.
Viola Davis became the first Black actress to achieve the Triple Crown of Acting, winning an Oscar, Emmy, and Tony Award.
No Country for Old Men (2008) won four Academy Awards, including Best Picture, despite its unconventional lack of a traditional musical score.
Mad Men's pilot episode was written by Matthew Weiner years before it was picked up, after being passed on by multiple networks.
Gone with the Wind (1939) held the record for highest-grossing film for over 25 years when adjusted for inflation.
Charlie Kaufman's screenplay for Being John Malkovich took several years to sell because studios found it too unconventional.
Cate Blanchett won Best Actress for Blue Jasmine (2013) and Best Supporting Actress for The Aviator (2004).
The Sundance Film Festival, founded by Robert Redford, has been the launchpad for countless independent drama films.
Succession's creator Jesse Armstrong previously wrote for British comedies including Peep Show and The Thick of It.
A Beautiful Mind (2001) won Best Picture despite controversy over its fictionalized portrayal of mathematician John Nash.
Robert De Niro gained 60 pounds to play Jake LaMotta in the later scenes of Raging Bull (1980).
Emmanuel Lubezki won three consecutive Best Cinematography Oscars for Gravity, Birdman, and The Revenant.
Spike Lee's Do the Right Thing (1989) was controversially snubbed for Best Picture at the Academy Awards.
One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest (1975) was only the second film to win all five major Academy Awards: Picture, Director, Actor, Actress, and Screenplay.
The Social Network (2010) was written by Aaron Sorkin and directed by David Fincher, creating one of the definitive films about Silicon Valley.
Ozark was originally developed as a film project before being reimagined as a television series for Netflix.
To Kill a Mockingbird (1962) earned Gregory Peck the Academy Award for Best Actor for his portrayal of Atticus Finch.
Frances McDormand has won three Best Actress Oscars: for Fargo, Three Billboards Outside Ebbing Missouri, and Nomadland.
The Cannes Film Festival's top prize, the Palme d'Or, is one of the most prestigious awards in world cinema.
Whiplash (2014) began as a short film that was used to attract funding for the full feature version.
Al Pacino was nominated for eight Academy Awards before finally winning for Scent of a Woman in 1993.
Barry Jenkins directed Moonlight using a two-camera setup to create a more intimate and naturalistic feel.
The Imitation Game (2014) depicts Alan Turing's work cracking the Enigma code during World War II, though it took significant dramatic liberties.
Friday Night Lights transitioned from a book to a film to a critically acclaimed TV series, each version finding its own audience.
Greta Gerwig became only the fifth woman nominated for Best Director at the Academy Awards for Lady Bird (2017).
Walk the Line (2005) required Joaquin Phoenix and Reese Witherspoon to perform all of their own vocals.
The Venice Film Festival, established in 1932, is the oldest film festival in the world.
There Will Be Blood (2007) features Daniel Day-Lewis in a towering performance as oil prospector Daniel Plainview.
This Is Us was known for its non-linear storytelling, frequently jumping between different time periods within a single episode.
Sofia Coppola won the Academy Award for Best Original Screenplay for Lost in Translation (2003).
Bohemian Rhapsody (2018) became the highest-grossing musical biographical film of all time.
Manchester by the Sea (2016) was written and directed by Kenneth Lonergan and earned Casey Affleck the Best Actor Oscar.
The Godfather Part II (1974) is one of the rare sequels considered equal to or better than the original film.
Ray (2004) earned Jamie Foxx the Academy Award for Best Actor for his portrayal of musician Ray Charles.
John Williams has been nominated for over 50 Academy Awards, making him the most nominated living person in Oscar history.
Marriage Story (2019) was inspired by writer-director Noah Baumbach's own divorce experience.
Lincoln (2012) focused on the final four months of Abraham Lincoln's life and the passage of the 13th Amendment.
Breaking Bad creator Vince Gilligan originally considered setting the show in Riverside, California before choosing Albuquerque for its tax incentives.
The Power of the Dog (2021) earned Jane Campion the Academy Award for Best Director.
Elvis (2022) starred Austin Butler, who stayed in character for so long that his natural accent was temporarily altered.
Quentin Tarantino has stated he plans to retire after directing his tenth film to preserve the quality of his filmography.
Nomadland (2020) used real nomads as cast members alongside Frances McDormand, blurring the line between documentary and fiction.
King Richard (2021) told the story of Venus and Serena Williams' father, earning Will Smith the Best Actor Oscar.
The Shawshank Redemption was adapted from Stephen King's novella 'Rita Hayworth and Shawshank Redemption.'
Mad Men meticulously recreated 1960s New York, with the production design team sourcing authentic vintage props and furnishings.
Oppenheimer (2023) was shot using a combination of IMAX 65mm and large-format film, with some scenes in black and white.
Roger Deakins has served as cinematographer on multiple Coen Brothers films, including Fargo and No Country for Old Men.
Citizen Kane pioneered deep focus cinematography, allowing objects in both the foreground and background to remain in sharp focus.
The Sopranos finale divided audiences with its abrupt cut to black, which remains one of TV's most debated endings.
Meryl Streep learned to play the violin for Music of the Heart and mastered an Australian accent for Evil Angels.
Casablanca's famous line 'Here's looking at you, kid' was partially improvised by Humphrey Bogart.
Succession drew heavily on real-world media dynasties like the Murdoch family for its depiction of the Roy family.
The Academy Awards ceremony was first held in 1929 at the Hollywood Roosevelt Hotel, lasting only 15 minutes.
Parasite director Bong Joon-ho meticulously storyboarded the entire film before production began.
Daniel Day-Lewis reportedly stayed in a wheelchair on set during the filming of My Left Foot (1989) and had crew members feed him.
Better Call Saul's Rhea Seehorn was never nominated for an Emmy for her role as Kim Wexler, widely considered one of TV's biggest snubs.
Martin Scorsese's Goodfellas features a famous unbroken tracking shot following Henry Hill through the Copacabana nightclub.
Schindler's List was shot in 72 days on location in Kraków, Poland, near the actual sites depicted in the film.
The Wire is often cited by critics and academics as the greatest television drama ever produced.
Hans Zimmer created the score for The Dark Knight using unconventional sounds, including razor blades on piano wire.
Gone with the Wind was the first color film to win the Academy Award for Best Picture.
David Fincher's Zodiac (2007) was one of the first major Hollywood films shot primarily on digital cameras.
Denzel Washington directed and starred in Fences (2016), adapting August Wilson's Pulitzer Prize-winning play.
The Godfather's cat, held by Marlon Brando in the opening scene, was a stray found on the Paramount lot.
A Beautiful Mind depicted the life of Nobel laureate John Nash, who struggled with schizophrenia for decades.
Moonlight cinematographer James Laxton used specific color palettes for each of the film's three chapters.
Spike Lee funded his debut feature She's Gotta Have It (1986) with money from grants and credit cards.
Cate Blanchett played Bob Dylan in I'm Not There (2007), one of six actors portraying different aspects of the musician.
The Social Network's rowing race sequence at the Henley Royal Regatta was actually filmed at multiple locations and digitally composited.
Ozark's final season was split into two parts, a trend that became common with prestige television dramas.
12 Angry Men was directed by Sidney Lumet, who went on to direct classics like Dog Day Afternoon and Network.
Frances McDormand famously refused to campaign for her Oscar wins, letting her performances speak for themselves.
Ennio Morricone did not win a competitive Academy Award until The Hateful Eight (2015), despite his legendary career.
The Crown's production budget was reported to be over $100 million for its first two seasons combined.
Aaron Sorkin's screenplay for A Few Good Men originated as a play he wrote on cocktail napkins while bartending.
Whiplash director Damien Chazelle went on to direct La La Land and become the youngest Best Director winner at age 32.
Al Pacino and Robert De Niro appeared together on screen for the first time in Heat (1995), despite both starring in The Godfather Part II.
Barry Jenkins's follow-up to Moonlight was If Beale Street Could Talk, adapted from James Baldwin's novel.
No Country for Old Men's villain Anton Chigurh was ranked as one of the greatest movie villains of all time by the AFI.
Greta Gerwig's Little Women (2019) restructured Louisa May Alcott's novel with a non-linear narrative.
The Imitation Game earned Benedict Cumberbatch his first Academy Award nomination for Best Actor.
Friday Night Lights used a documentary-style shooting approach with handheld cameras and natural lighting.
Charlie Kaufman wrote and directed Synecdoche, New York (2008), which Roger Ebert called the best film of the decade.
Robert De Niro co-founded the Tribeca Film Festival in 2002 to revitalize Lower Manhattan after September 11.
There Will Be Blood's famous 'I drink your milkshake' scene was inspired by actual testimony from the Teapot Dome scandal.
Viola Davis's performance in Fences earned her the Academy Award for Best Supporting Actress.
This Is Us creator Dan Fogelman originally pitched the show as a feature film before reimagining it as a TV series.
Sofia Coppola was the first American woman nominated for the Academy Award for Best Director.
Bohemian Rhapsody was plagued by production issues, including the firing and replacement of director Bryan Singer during filming.
Manchester by the Sea was originally set to star Matt Damon, who transitioned to a producer role.
The Godfather Part II features parallel storylines showing young Vito Corleone's rise and Michael Corleone's consolidation of power.
Walk the Line's Reese Witherspoon won her first and only Academy Award for playing June Carter Cash.
Emmanuel Lubezki shot The Revenant using only natural light, often limiting filming to brief windows at dawn and dusk.
Marriage Story features a devastating argument scene between Adam Driver and Scarlett Johansson that was filmed in one continuous take.
Lincoln's screenplay by Tony Kushner was adapted from Doris Kearns Goodwin's book Team of Rivals.
Oppenheimer earned Robert Downey Jr. his first Academy Award for his role as Lewis Strauss.
Breaking Bad's Bryan Cranston was primarily known as a comedic actor from Malcolm in the Middle before his dramatic transformation.
The Power of the Dog was adapted from Thomas Savage's 1967 novel of the same name.
Nomadland director Chloé Zhao became the second woman and first woman of color to win Best Director at the Oscars.
King Richard was filmed partially during the COVID-19 pandemic, requiring extensive safety protocols on set.
Quentin Tarantino wrote the screenplay for Pulp Fiction in Amsterdam, drawing inspiration from European crime films.
Mad Men's Jon Hamm auditioned for the role of Don Draper after years of relative obscurity in Hollywood.
Citizen Kane was a commercial failure upon release due to a campaign by newspaper magnate William Randolph Hearst to suppress it.
John Williams composed the iconic themes for Schindler's List, which featured violin solos performed by Itzhak Perlman.
The Sopranos ran for six seasons from 1999 to 2007, totaling 86 episodes.
Meryl Streep's performance in Sophie's Choice (1982) required her to learn Polish and German for the role.
Parasite's set for the Park family house was entirely built from scratch on a studio lot in South Korea.
Succession was renewed for what became its final fourth season after winning the Emmy for Outstanding Drama Series.
Casablanca won the Academy Award for Best Picture at the 16th Academy Awards in 1944.
Daniel Day-Lewis retired from acting after Phantom Thread (2017), though he had previously announced retirement before.
The Wire's fifth and final season focused on the media, specifically the Baltimore Sun newspaper.
Schindler's List used over 20,000 extras during its production in Poland.
Martin Scorsese served as a producer on Joker (2019) before departing the project early in development.
Better Call Saul utilized a distinctive visual style with wide angles and geometric compositions inspired by classic westerns.
One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest was filmed at the Oregon State Hospital, an actual psychiatric facility.
Gone with the Wind's search for the actress to play Scarlett O'Hara became one of the most publicized casting calls in Hollywood history.
David Fincher directed the first two episodes of House of Cards, helping establish Netflix as a serious player in original content.
Denzel Washington's performance in Malcolm X (1992) is considered one of the greatest biographical performances in cinema history.
The Shawshank Redemption was nominated for seven Academy Awards but did not win any.
Hans Zimmer's score for Interstellar prominently featured a church organ recorded at Temple Church in London.
Moonlight was famously announced incorrectly as the Best Picture loser when La La Land was read by mistake at the 2017 Oscars.
Spike Lee's BlacKkKlansman (2018) earned him his first competitive Academy Award for Best Adapted Screenplay.
The Social Network earned eight Academy Award nominations and won three, including Best Adapted Screenplay.
Ozark's Jason Bateman directed multiple episodes of the series, earning Emmy nominations for both acting and directing.
Cate Blanchett served as the president of the Cannes Film Festival jury in 2018.
12 Angry Men was originally a teleplay written by Reginald Rose for the CBS television series Studio One.
Aaron Sorkin made his directorial debut with Molly's Game (2017), based on the true story of Molly Bloom's underground poker empire.
Roger Deakins transitioned from film to digital cinematography, shooting Skyfall on digital for the first time in the Bond franchise.
The Crown's first season depicted the early reign of Queen Elizabeth II, beginning with her marriage to Prince Philip.
Frances McDormand found the screenplay for Fargo while her husband Joel Coen was developing it with his brother Ethan.
Whiplash was filmed in just 19 days on a budget of $3.3 million.
Al Pacino's audition for The Godfather was initially rejected by Paramount executives who wanted a taller, more traditionally handsome actor.
Barry Jenkins and cinematographer James Laxton have been collaborators since their days at Florida State University's film school.
There Will Be Blood and No Country for Old Men were both released in 2007 and competed against each other at the Oscars.
Greta Gerwig starred in several mumblecore films before transitioning to mainstream directing.
The Imitation Game was partially funded by the UK Film Council and filmed primarily in England.
Friday Night Lights' famous motto 'Clear eyes, full hearts, can't lose' became a cultural catchphrase.
Ennio Morricone and director Sergio Leone were elementary school classmates in Rome.
Bohemian Rhapsody's Live Aid concert recreation was praised for its accuracy in replicating the original 1985 performance.
Manchester by the Sea was partially funded by Amazon Studios, making it one of the first streaming-backed films to win major Oscars.
The Godfather trilogy spans the years 1901 to 1997 across its three films and various flashback sequences.
Ray Charles personally approved Jamie Foxx to portray him before Charles passed away in 2004.
Sofia Coppola's Marie Antoinette (2006) featured a deliberately anachronistic soundtrack with post-punk and new wave music.
Marriage Story's custody battle scenes were informed by consultations with real divorce attorneys in New York and Los Angeles.
Lincoln was the last film scored by composer John Williams for a Steven Spielberg drama before their work on The Fabelmans.
Oppenheimer used practical effects rather than CGI to recreate the Trinity nuclear test explosion.
The Power of the Dog was filmed entirely in New Zealand, standing in for 1920s Montana.
Nomadland's Frances McDormand lived in a van during parts of the production to prepare for her role.
King Richard depicted Richard Williams' unconventional coaching methods, including a 78-page plan written before his daughters were born.
Quentin Tarantino worked at a video rental store before breaking into filmmaking, which heavily influenced his encyclopedic film knowledge.
Mad Men's final episode aired in May 2015 after seven seasons chronicling the advertising industry through the 1960s.
Citizen Kane was ranked as the greatest American film by the AFI for decades before being displaced by Vertigo on the Sight & Sound poll.
The Sopranos' James Gandolfini earned three Emmy Awards for Outstanding Lead Actor in a Drama Series.
Meryl Streep won her third Academy Award for The Iron Lady (2011), playing British Prime Minister Margaret Thatcher.
Parasite featured an original score by Jung Jae-il that blended classical music with contemporary Korean sounds.
Succession filmed its final season partly in Norway, standing in for various European locations.
Casablanca was rushed into release to coincide with the Allied invasion of North Africa in November 1942.
Daniel Day-Lewis apprenticed as a cobbler in Italy between film roles to immerse himself in a completely different craft.
Better Call Saul's timeline spans from the early 2000s through the Breaking Bad era and into the post-Breaking Bad future.
The Wire cast many non-professional actors from Baltimore, including real former drug dealers and police officers.
Schindler's List grossed over $322 million worldwide, with Spielberg donating his profits to the Shoah Foundation.
Martin Scorsese's The Irishman (2019) used groundbreaking de-aging visual effects on its three lead actors.
Gone with the Wind featured Hattie McDaniel, who became the first African American to win an Academy Award.
David Fincher directed the music video for Madonna's 'Express Yourself' before transitioning to feature films.
Denzel Washington has directed four films, including Antwone Fisher, The Great Debaters, Fences, and A Journal for Jordan.
The Shawshank Redemption's filming location, the Ohio State Reformatory, is now a popular tourist destination.
Hans Zimmer developed a new synthesizer technique for the Dune score, creating sounds meant to evoke an alien world.
Moonlight was adapted from Tarell Alvin McCraney's unpublished play 'In Moonlight Black Boys Look Blue.'
Spike Lee's 25th Hour (2002) was the first major Hollywood film to address the aftermath of September 11.
The Social Network's Jesse Eisenberg and Andrew Garfield reportedly did not get along during filming, which enhanced their on-screen tension.
Ozark was filmed primarily in the Lake Allatoona area of Georgia, not the actual Ozarks region of Missouri.
12 Angry Men's Henry Fonda co-produced the film through his production company, investing his own money.
Cate Blanchett starred in Tár (2022), playing a fictional conductor in a film that blurred the line between drama and psychological thriller.
Aaron Sorkin's The Trial of the Chicago 7 was originally written in 2007 with Steven Spielberg attached to direct.
Roger Deakins has said that he prefers to use lighting to create mood rather than relying on color correction in post-production.
The Crown was created by Peter Morgan, who previously wrote The Queen (2006) and Frost/Nixon (2008).
Whiplash's J.K. Simmons won the Academy Award for Best Supporting Actor, completing a sweep of all major critics' awards that season.
Al Pacino turned down the lead role in Star Wars, Apocalypse Now, and several other major films during the peak of his career.
Francis Ford Coppola directed The Godfather, The Godfather Part II, The Conversation, and Apocalypse Now within a single decade.
There Will Be Blood's final scene was filmed inside a bowling alley, with Daniel Day-Lewis delivering the iconic milkshake monologue.
Greta Gerwig's Barbie (2023) became a massive commercial success while also being a thoughtful commentary on gender expectations.
The Imitation Game grossed over $233 million worldwide against a production budget of $14 million.
Friday Night Lights was filmed in Austin, Texas, using local residents as extras throughout the series.
Charlie Kaufman's Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind won the Academy Award for Best Original Screenplay.
Bohemian Rhapsody earned Rami Malek the Academy Award for Best Actor for his portrayal of Freddie Mercury.
Manchester by the Sea's script was praised for its realistic depiction of grief, avoiding typical Hollywood emotional catharsis.
The Godfather's wedding scene at the beginning of the film was shot over several days with hundreds of extras.
Walk the Line was released in 2005 and earned over $186 million worldwide against a $28 million budget.
Emmanuel Lubezki's long tracking shots in Children of Men (2006) are considered some of the greatest technical achievements in modern cinema.
Viola Davis delivered a powerful speech about opportunity for women of color when accepting her Emmy for How to Get Away with Murder.
Marriage Story received six Academy Award nominations and won Best Supporting Actress for Laura Dern.
Lincoln featured a cast of over 140 speaking roles, one of the largest dramatic ensembles in modern film history.
Breaking Bad's final episode drew 10.3 million viewers, making it one of the most-watched cable series finales.
The Power of the Dog's Benedict Cumberbatch learned to play banjo and roll cigarettes with one hand for his role.
Nomadland won the Golden Lion at the Venice Film Festival before going on to win Best Picture at the Oscars.
King Richard's Will Smith slapped presenter Chris Rock at the 2022 Academy Awards ceremony shortly before winning Best Actor.
Quentin Tarantino's films are known for their non-linear narratives, sharp dialogue, and frequent references to pop culture.
Citizen Kane introduced several innovative filmmaking techniques, including low-angle shots that showed ceilings on set for the first time.
The Sopranos was created by David Chase, who drew on his own Italian-American upbringing in New Jersey.
Meryl Streep's audition for The Deer Hunter (1978) was her breakthrough, despite the role being relatively small.
Parasite's production design team built the entire lower-class Kim family home as a set that could be flooded for the rain sequence.
Succession won the Emmy for Outstanding Drama Series in 2020 and 2022.
Daniel Day-Lewis learned to track and skin animals for The Last of the Mohicans (1992) and subsisted on what he hunted.
Better Call Saul's Bob Odenkirk suffered a heart attack during filming of the final season but returned to complete the show.
The Wire's fourth season, focusing on the education system, is widely regarded as the greatest single season of television.
Martin Scorsese's Taxi Driver (1976) was inspired by the loneliness and alienation of life in New York City.
Gone with the Wind ran for nearly four hours and required an intermission during theatrical screenings.
David Fincher's Se7en (1995) was notable for its dark, desaturated visual style, achieved through a special film processing technique.
The Shawshank Redemption's director Frank Darabont also directed The Green Mile, another Stephen King prison adaptation.
Hans Zimmer won his first Academy Award for Best Original Score for The Lion King (1994).
Spike Lee's Malcolm X (1992) ran over 200 minutes, with Warner Bros. initially refusing to fund the extended runtime.
Ozark's Julia Garner won three consecutive Emmy Awards for Outstanding Supporting Actress for her role as Ruth Langmore.
A Beautiful Mind's Russell Crowe was nominated for Best Actor in three consecutive years: The Insider, Gladiator, and A Beautiful Mind.
Cate Blanchett and director Todd Haynes collaborated on Carol (2015), which earned six Academy Award nominations.
The Crown's Matt Smith, Olivia Colman, and Imelda Staunton each portrayed Queen Elizabeth II across different decades.
Aaron Sorkin wrote the screenplay for Steve Jobs (2015) as a three-act structure, with each act taking place before a product launch.
Roger Deakins shot 1917 (2019) to appear as one continuous take, using carefully choreographed camera movements.
Whiplash explores the abusive teacher-student dynamic between a jazz drumming student and his ruthless instructor.
Al Pacino returned to Broadway multiple times throughout his career, winning Tony Awards for Does a Tiger Wear a Necktie? and The Basic Training of Pavlo Hummel.
There Will Be Blood was shot primarily in Marfa, Texas, with its desolate landscape standing in for early 20th century California oil fields.
Greta Gerwig acted in Frances Ha (2012), which she co-wrote with her partner Noah Baumbach.
The Imitation Game's screenplay by Graham Moore won the Academy Award for Best Adapted Screenplay.
Friday Night Lights' Kyle Chandler won the Emmy for Outstanding Lead Actor in a Drama Series for his role as Coach Eric Taylor.
Ennio Morricone received an honorary Academy Award in 2007 for his contributions to the art of film music.
Manchester by the Sea takes place in the real town of Manchester-by-the-Sea, Massachusetts.
The Godfather was adapted from Mario Puzo's bestselling 1969 novel of the same name.
Francis Ford Coppola's daughter Sofia had a controversial role in The Godfather Part III, receiving significant criticism.
Moonlight's three-part structure follows its protagonist Chiron through childhood, adolescence, and adulthood.
No Country for Old Men's Javier Bardem wore a specially designed pneumatic cattle gun as his character's signature weapon.
The Social Network was released in October 2010, just six years after Facebook was founded.
Robert De Niro drove a real taxi in New York City for several weeks to prepare for his role in Taxi Driver.
Viola Davis is one of the few performers to have won acting awards from all four major American entertainment ceremonies.
This Is Us was the most-watched broadcast network drama during its first season, averaging over 12 million viewers.
Sofia Coppola's The Virgin Suicides (1999) was her directorial debut, adapted from Jeffrey Eugenides' novel.
Bohemian Rhapsody's production team recreated Wembley Stadium's Live Aid stage to exact specifications.
Casablanca's script was famously being rewritten during filming, with actors often receiving their lines the same day they shot scenes.
Ray was initially developed with multiple directors attached before Taylor Hackford took the helm.
Oppenheimer was Christopher Nolan's first R-rated film since Insomnia in 2002.
The Power of the Dog earned 12 Academy Award nominations, the most for any film at the 2022 ceremony.
Quentin Tarantino's Reservoir Dogs (1992) was made for just $1.2 million and launched his career as a filmmaker.
Mad Men explored themes of identity, consumerism, and the American Dream through the lens of 1960s advertising culture.
12 Angry Men was shot in just 20 days on a modest budget and became a landmark of American cinema.
Charlie Kaufman wrote Adaptation (2002) after struggling to adapt Susan Orlean's book The Orchid Thief, turning his writer's block into the screenplay itself.
The Shawshank Redemption's Tim Robbins and Morgan Freeman were among 1,000 actors who auditioned for their respective roles.
Hans Zimmer collaborated with director Denis Villeneuve to create the otherworldly score for Dune using custom instruments.
The Wire was notable for not using any incidental music, relying solely on diegetic sound and end-of-season montages.
Meryl Streep's performance in Kramer vs. Kramer (1979) earned her the Academy Award for Best Supporting Actress.
Parasite was the first South Korean film to receive Academy Award nominations, ultimately winning four.
Succession's Jeremy Strong is known for his intense method approach to playing Kendall Roy.
Casablanca features the song 'As Time Goes By,' which was written by Herman Hupfeld in 1931, over a decade before the film.
Daniel Day-Lewis built a house using only 19th-century tools to prepare for his role in Gangs of New York.
Better Call Saul premiered in February 2015, attracting a record 6.9 million viewers for its debut episode.
Martin Scorsese founded The Film Foundation in 1990 to preserve and restore classic films.
Gone with the Wind's premiere in Atlanta, Georgia drew over 300,000 spectators to the city.
David Fincher's Fight Club (1999) was initially a box office disappointment but became a cultural phenomenon on home video.
Denzel Washington's son John David Washington starred in Tenet (2020) and BlacKkKlansman (2018).
Schindler's List features a girl in a red coat as the only color element in the otherwise black-and-white film.
One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest's Jack Nicholson spent time in a real mental institution observing patients before filming.
Breaking Bad is set in Albuquerque, New Mexico, and the actual house used as Walter White's home became a tourist attraction.
The Godfather Part II won six Academy Awards, including Best Picture and Best Supporting Actor for Robert De Niro.
Spike Lee named his production company 40 Acres and a Mule Filmworks, referencing a broken Reconstruction-era promise to freed slaves.
Cate Blanchett played dual roles in Coffee and Cigarettes (2003), directed by Jim Jarmusch.
Ozark premiered on Netflix in July 2017 and ran for four seasons through April 2022.
Roger Deakins received a knighthood from Queen Elizabeth II for his services to film in 2021.
Frances McDormand also produced Nomadland, making her one of few performers to win Best Picture and Best Actress for the same film.
Aaron Sorkin's dialogue in The West Wing averaged around 250 words per page more than the typical television script.
The Crown depicted the Aberfan disaster in its third season, an episode that was particularly emotionally devastating for viewers.
Whiplash's Miles Teller actually played the drums in the film and practiced for months to achieve the required skill level.
There Will Be Blood earned Daniel Day-Lewis his second Academy Award for Best Actor.
Greta Gerwig's Lady Bird received five Academy Award nominations and grossed over $79 million worldwide.
Friday Night Lights was based on H.G. Bissinger's 1990 book about high school football in Odessa, Texas.
Ennio Morricone's score for The Mission (1986) is considered one of the greatest film scores ever composed.
Bohemian Rhapsody grossed over $900 million worldwide, becoming one of the highest-grossing biopics in history.
The Social Network's opening scene at a bar was rehearsed 99 times before David Fincher was satisfied with the take.
No Country for Old Men was filmed in locations across New Mexico and Texas.
Al Pacino's role in Scarface (1983) was initially criticized but later became recognized as one of his most iconic performances.
Barry Jenkins studied film at Florida State University, where he was influenced by the works of Wong Kar-wai and Claire Denis.
The Imitation Game was released in November 2014, timing its release for awards season consideration.
Robert De Niro's performance in The Deer Hunter (1978) was one of the first major films to address the psychological impact of the Vietnam War.
Viola Davis's memoir 'Finding Me' became a New York Times bestseller and revealed her difficult upbringing.
This Is Us used makeup and prosthetics alongside digital de-aging to portray characters across decades.
Quentin Tarantino's Kill Bill was originally conceived as a single film before being split into two volumes.
Mad Men's final shot of Don Draper meditating at a retreat, followed by the 'I'd Like to Buy the World a Coke' commercial, sparked endless debate about its meaning.
Citizen Kane's 'Rosebud' reveal is one of the most famous plot twists in cinema history.
The Sopranos pioneered the antihero protagonist format that would define prestige television for the next two decades.
Manchester by the Sea's Kenneth Lonergan also wrote the play 'This Is Our Youth' and directed 'Margaret' (2011).
The Godfather was released on March 15, 1972, and became the highest-grossing film of that year.
Walk the Line's Joaquin Phoenix earned a Golden Globe for Best Actor in a Musical or Comedy for his performance.
Emmanuel Lubezki's work on The Tree of Life (2011) used natural light and handheld cameras to create an immersive visual experience.
Marriage Story was partially filmed in the same Brooklyn neighborhood where Noah Baumbach grew up.
Lincoln's Daniel Day-Lewis studied Lincoln's writings extensively and adopted a high-pitched voice historians believe Lincoln actually had.
Breaking Bad's 'Ozymandias' episode is rated as the highest-rated television episode on IMDb.
Moonlight cinematographer James Laxton shot the film on location in Miami's Liberty City neighborhood.
The Power of the Dog explores toxic masculinity and repressed identity in 1920s Montana.
Nomadland was based on Jessica Bruder's nonfiction book about older Americans living as modern-day nomads.
King Richard focused on the story before Venus and Serena Williams became famous, showing their journey from Compton.
Sofia Coppola won the Golden Lion at the Venice Film Festival for Somewhere (2010).
Oppenheimer ran approximately three hours, making it one of the longest films to achieve massive mainstream box office success.
Elvis director Baz Luhrmann is known for his maximalist visual style, previously seen in Moulin Rouge! and The Great Gatsby.
A Beautiful Mind won four Academy Awards at the 74th ceremony, including Best Picture and Best Director for Ron Howard.
Schindler's List was originally going to be directed by Roman Polanski, who declined because the subject matter was too personal.
The Crown's sixth and final season covered the period from the mid-1990s to the early 2000s.
12 Angry Men was remade as a television film in 1997 with Jack Lemmon in the Henry Fonda role.
Charlie Kaufman's screenplay for Being John Malkovich earned him his first Academy Award nomination.
Casablanca director Michael Curtiz was a Hungarian-born filmmaker who directed over 100 films during his career.
Francis Ford Coppola's wine business, which he started in the 1970s, became as well-known as his filmmaking career.
The Shawshank Redemption's iconic scene of Andy Dufresne in the rain after his escape was filmed amid actual thunderstorms.

The Wire's Omar Little, played by Michael K. Williams, was once called the greatest character in television by President Obama.
Meryl Streep has been nominated for Golden Globe Awards more times than any other actor in history.
Martin Scorsese considered becoming a priest before pursuing filmmaking, studying at a seminary as a teenager.
Better Call Saul's visual storytelling often used point-of-view shots from inside objects like refrigerators and car trunks.
One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest was produced by Michael Douglas early in his career, before he became a major leading man.
Gone with the Wind required three directors: George Cukor was replaced by Victor Fleming, who was briefly replaced by Sam Wood.
David Fincher's Mank (2020) was shot entirely in black and white and told the story of Citizen Kane screenwriter Herman J. Mankiewicz.
Denzel Washington's Glory (1989) earned him his first Oscar and featured one of cinema's most powerful emotional scenes.

No Country for Old Men was adapted from Cormac McCarthy's 2005 novel by Joel and Ethan Coen.
The Social Network's Justin Timberlake played Napster co-founder Sean Parker and received strong critical praise.

Cate Blanchett won the Volpi Cup for Best Actress at the Venice Film Festival for her role in Tár.
Aaron Sorkin's The Social Network screenplay was adapted from Ben Mezrich's book 'The Accidental Billionaires.'
Roger Deakins and the Coen Brothers have collaborated on over a dozen films together.
Frances McDormand met her husband Joel Coen when she auditioned for Blood Simple (1984), his directorial debut.
Whiplash was inspired by director Damien Chazelle's own experience as a competitive jazz drummer in high school.
Al Pacino's performance in Dog Day Afternoon (1975) was based on the true story of John Wojtowicz's bank robbery.
There Will Be Blood's score by Radiohead guitarist Jonny Greenwood was partially disqualified from Oscar consideration because some music was pre-existing.
The Godfather earned three Academy Awards: Best Picture, Best Actor for Marlon Brando, and Best Adapted Screenplay.
Greta Gerwig is one of the few filmmakers to have directed two films that each grossed over $100 million domestically.

Ennio Morricone composed the score for The Untouchables (1987), featuring one of cinema's most recognizable main themes.

Manchester by the Sea premiered at the Sundance Film Festival before its wide theatrical release.
The Godfather Part II is the only sequel to win the Academy Award for Best Picture.
Moonlight's Mahershala Ali became the first Muslim actor to win an Academy Award for his role in the film.
Marriage Story was released by Netflix and received widespread theatrical distribution before streaming.

Oppenheimer earned 13 Academy Award nominations and won seven, including Best Picture and Best Director.
Elvis's Austin Butler reportedly continued speaking with a Memphis accent for months after filming wrapped.

A Beautiful Mind used visual techniques to blur the line between Nash's reality and his hallucinations, keeping audiences guessing.

The Sopranos filmed primarily in New Jersey, using real locations in Newark, Kearny, and various North Jersey suburbs.

The Shawshank Redemption was released on September 23, 1994.
Parasite's director Bong Joon-ho described his film as a 'staircase movie' because stairs serve as a metaphor for class throughout.
The Crown received critical acclaim for its lavish production design and meticulous attention to historical detail.
Hans Zimmer's time-bending score for Dunkirk used a Shepard tone, an auditory illusion of constantly ascending pitch.
Casablanca was not expected to be a particularly notable film during production, as it was one of dozens of films Warner Bros. produced that year.
Nomadland premiered at the Venice Film Festival in September 2020.
Denzel Washington has been nominated for ten Academy Awards, winning two.
Mad Men premiered on AMC in July 2007, the same month the network also launched the drama series Breaking Bad's development.

Meryl Streep and Al Pacino appeared together in Angels in America (2003), both receiving Emmy nominations.
Robert De Niro reportedly considered over 100 scripts before agreeing to star in Silver Linings Playbook.
The Wire was created by David Simon and former Baltimore homicide detective Ed Burns.
Spike Lee's She's Gotta Have It was remade as a Netflix series that ran for two seasons from 2017 to 2019.
